title: dianthus carthusianorum subsp. carthusianorum
intro: Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. carthusianorum, also known as Monk's Reserve or Charters' Pink, is a perennial plant species of the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Dianthus carthusianorum has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to offer various health benefits, including relief from gastrointestinal issues, coughs, and skin irritations, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Dianthus carthusianorum can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
